customers.name: Mayor / Deputy Mayor
customers.description: An elected municipal leader accountable for visible quality of life, resident trust, budget discipline, and public legitimacy when councils respond to illegal dumping and neighborhood nuisance.
careAbout: Visible reduction in illegal dumping and nuisance hotspots
Showing residents that public action is fast and proportionate
Avoiding politically risky surveillance or privacy controversies
Reducing cleanup costs and recovering more fines
Deploying quickly without roadworks or long infrastructure projects
winsThem: A deployment that starts in days rather than months
Clear proof of incident reduction and enforcement action
Dashboards that tie field action to resident-visible outcomes
A path to expand from waste enforcement into broader safety use cases
theirFear: Installing technology that looks intrusive or legally fragile
Spending public money without visible outcomes on the ground
Creating a field system that adds complexity instead of reducing burden

jobsToBeDone.name: Reduce visible public-space nuisance without intrusive surveillance
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Target recurring dumping and nuisance zones with fast, proportionate deployments that create enforceable action while protecting resident privacy.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: The council can show cleaner public spaces, faster intervention, and stronger resident confidence without creating a surveillance backlash.

steps.step: Choose the right hotspots to instrument first
steps.description: Focus rollout on places where nuisance is recurrent, politically visible, and operationally expensive.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Deployment Planning and Site Selection
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Helps councils prioritize the sites where deployment is most likely to reduce incidents and show impact.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Geospatial Incident Map
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows incident concentration, recurrence, and intervention history across the territory.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor JTBD step 1: Choose the right hotspots to instrument first
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Reduce visible public-space nuisance without intrusive surveillance": Choose the right hotspots to instrument first.

steps.description: Install movable devices and compliant signage without civil works or long setup delays.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Edge Camera and Sensor Unit
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the mobile field capture surface that can be installed and moved quickly.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Secure 4G/5G Connectivity Gateway
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps deployments independent from fiber and heavy infrastructure.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Resident Communication and Signage Kit
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes deployments visible, legible, and aligned with public information obligations.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Reduce visible public-space nuisance without intrusive surveillance": Deploy quickly with visible safeguards.

steps.step: Show that the council acted and results changed
steps.description: Track incident reduction, enforcement outcomes, and operational savings in a way elected teams can communicate publicly.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Executive Impact Dashboard
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Turns operations data into a council-level story about outcomes, not just alerts.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Outcome Analytics and Benchmarking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Measures reductions, recurrence, and comparative performance across sites and periods.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor JTBD step 3: Show that the council acted and results changed
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Reduce visible public-space nuisance without intrusive surveillance": Show that the council acted and results changed.

jobsToBeDone.name: Defend the investment politically and financially
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Demonstrate that the enforcement program improves quality of life, reduces service cost, and stands up to privacy and legal scrutiny.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Municipal leaders can justify expansion, budget renewal, and cross-service adoption with credible operational evidence.

steps.description: Tie enforcement and faster cleanup to avoided cost, recovered fines, and improved service leverage.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Fine Recovery and Treasury Integration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tracks the financial outcome of cases and collected penalties.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Field Service Task Dispatch
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows how faster task routing reduces wasted field effort and repeat visits.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Defend the investment politically and financially": Prove financial and service value.

steps.description: Show that privacy zoning, retention, and access rules are designed into the system, not added later.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: DPIA and GDPR Compliance Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Packages the legal, DPIA, and governance material councils need for accountable deployment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Access Control and Audit Trail
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides traceability over who viewed evidence, when, and why.

customerJourneyStories.name: Visible cleanup with political confidence
customerJourneyStories.summary: A mayor wants a fast and publicly defensible response to repeated dumping hotspots without creating the optics or legal exposure of indiscriminate surveillance.

stages.narrative: Resident complaints, local press pressure, or repeated cleanup costs make a hotspot politically urgent.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Visible cleanup with political confidence": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The mayor looks for a solution that works quickly, protects privacy, and shows visible impact before the next budget cycle.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Visible cleanup with political confidence": Discovery.

stages.narrative: They compare rollout speed, legal defensibility, municipal references, and whether results are measurable beyond anecdotal success.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Visible cleanup with political confidence": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: A limited deployment is approved on a few priority sites with close scrutiny on legality, reaction from residents, and first case throughput.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Visible cleanup with political confidence": Trial.

stages.narrative: The council expands when incidents fall, enforcement becomes visible, and the administrative burden on services drops.

media.title: Mayor / Deputy Mayor journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Visible cleanup with political confidence": Engagement.

stages.narrative: Vizzia remains strategic when leadership can defend renewals through resident impact, economic return, and credible compliance.

media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Visible cleanup with political confidence": Retention.

media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Visible cleanup with political confidence" for customer "Mayor / Deputy Mayor".

top.name: Public-space nuisance reduction
top.description: Cut confirmed fly-tipping incidents in instrumented zones from 14.2 to 5.0 per zone per month.

children.name: Repeat incident rate
children.description: Lower repeat dumping on previously cleaned hotspots from 38% to 15%.
children.unit: % of cleaned hotspots with recurrence in 30 days

children.name: Resident complaint response time
children.description: Reduce the average time from resident complaint to visible action from 9.4 to 3.0 days.

children.name: Incidents leading to visible action
children.description: Increase the share of qualified incidents with a recorded intervention from 46% to 80%.

children.name: Resident cleanliness confidence score
children.description: Improve the resident confidence score on municipal cleanliness from 58 to 72.

top.name: Municipal value realization
top.description: Move the council's combined enforcement value index from 61 to 82 through lower cleanup cost and higher recoveries.

children.name: Fines collected
children.description: Increase annual fines collected on instrumented cases from EUR 0.48M to EUR 1.10M.

children.name: Cleanup cost avoided
children.description: Increase annual cleanup cost avoided from EUR 0.92M to EUR 1.80M.

children.name: Officer admin hours per case
children.description: Reduce average administrative work per enforcement case from 1.8 to 0.8 hours.

children.name: Days from deployment to first actionable case
children.description: Shorten time-to-first-case from 12 to 5 days after site activation.

productStrategy.vision: Make public-space enforcement feel fast, visible, lawful, and politically defensible for local leaders.

1_year.focus: Win waste-enforcement budgets through visible impact and compliance confidence.

customerKPI.northStar: Reduce confirmed dumping incidents by at least 50% on instrumented hotspots
supporting: Resident complaint response time under 4 days
Incidents leading to visible action above 70%

businessKPI.northStar: Annual municipal value realization index above 75

milestones: Budget-renewal proof packs used across the installed base

3_year.focus: Expand from dumping hotspots into broader municipal nuisance and safety coordination.

customerKPI.northStar: Most councils run one shared public-space operations model across waste and safety alerts

businessKPI.northStar: Expansion revenue from adjacent municipal workflows exceeds new-logo device revenue
supporting: More municipalities expanding to second use case

5_year.focus: Become the standard operating layer for compliant public-space offence response in European municipalities.

customerKPI.northStar: Councils manage most recurring public-space offence categories in one accountable platform
supporting: Resident trust remains stable as deployment footprint grows
Administrative workload per case stays low at scale

businessKPI.northStar: High-retention municipal platform revenue with multi-country operating leverage

milestones: Common compliance framework reused across markets
Shared municipal enforcement data model becomes the platform moat

customers.name: Head of Local Police
customers.description: Leader of municipal police or enforcement operations responsible for turning incidents into legally usable cases while protecting officer time, procedural quality, and coordination across field teams.
careAbout: Building enforceable cases from remote detections
Reducing paperwork and repeat handling per incident
Giving officers clear prioritization across hotspots
Maintaining chain of custody and access control
Coordinating quickly with technical services and partner authorities
winsThem: Remote triage that filters noise before officers intervene
Evidence packs that are already aligned with legal procedure
Mobile workflows that keep case progress moving from the field
Full traceability over who accessed footage and what changed
Integration into existing municipal and interagency processes
theirFear: Collecting evidence that later fails legal review
Overloading officers with alerts that do not turn into cases
Losing control over retention, access rights, or evidentiary traceability

jobsToBeDone.name: Turn a field alert into an enforceable case quickly
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Qualify an alert, secure admissible evidence, and generate the procedural material needed to move the case forward without rebuilding the file manually.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: More incidents become enforceable cases while officers spend less time assembling and documenting them.

steps.description: Separate real offences from noise before officers spend time on the case.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Incident Detection Rules Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies detection logic tuned to nuisance and offence patterns.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: AI-assisted Incident Triage
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Prioritizes likely actionable events for operator review.

media.title: Head of Local Police JTBD step 1: Review only credible incidents
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Turn a field alert into an enforceable case quickly": Review only credible incidents.

steps.description: Preserve the right snapshots, timestamps, and metadata with privacy boundaries already applied.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Evidence Vault and Chain of Custody
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Stores admissible evidence with integrity and access history.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Privacy Zoning and Masking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps captures focused on relevant public areas and avoids sensitive private zones.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Access Control and Audit Trail
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tracks all access and case actions for procedural defensibility.

media.title: Head of Local Police JTBD step 2: Secure evidence and legal context
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Turn a field alert into an enforceable case quickly": Secure evidence and legal context.

steps.description: Prepare the case, notice, and follow-up material needed for municipal action or external transfer.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Enforcement Case Workspace
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Organizes the incident, people, evidence, and process state in one case record.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Legal Procedure Templates
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps case progression aligned with the local legal procedure.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Notice and Fine Pack Generator
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Produces the formal package required for notification and sanction.

jobsToBeDone.outcome: Hotspots are handled faster and recurrence is reduced because field action, cleanup, and enforcement follow one coordinated plan.

steps.description: Focus scarce enforcement time on hotspots with the strongest recurrence, visibility, or case probability.

top.name: Enforceable case throughput
top.description: Increase qualified incidents converted into enforceable case files from 18 to 55 per month.

children.name: Evidence admissibility rate
children.description: Increase the share of reviewed cases that meet evidentiary standards from 71% to 92%.

children.name: Cases missing procedural elements
children.description: Reduce incomplete case packs from 22% to 5%.

children.name: Time from alert to case qualification
children.description: Reduce median alert-to-qualification time from 19 to 6 hours.

children.name: Officer time spent per incident
children.description: Reduce average officer handling time from 54 to 24 minutes per incident.

top.name: Enforcement operating leverage
top.description: Raise enforcement operating leverage from 49 to 76 by increasing case yield without growing headcount.

children.name: Qualified incidents per officer-day
children.description: Increase productive qualified incidents handled per officer-day from 3.2 to 6.5.

children.name: Manual document preparation hours
children.description: Reduce monthly manual case-document preparation from 96 to 32 hours.

children.name: Partner handoff success rate
children.description: Increase first-pass successful handoff to partner services from 63% to 90%.

children.name: Repeat hotspot escalation delay
children.description: Reduce time to escalate persistent hotspots from 11 to 3 days.

productStrategy.vision: Make compliant municipal enforcement faster than today's paperwork-heavy default.

1_year.focus: Increase case yield from waste enforcement pilots and installed councils.

customerKPI.northStar: More than 40 enforceable cases per month in mature deployments

milestones: Partner handoff package adopted across core municipalities

3_year.focus: Generalize the case core across multiple offence categories and municipal partners.

customerKPI.northStar: One shared case model across waste and adjacent safety workflows

businessKPI.northStar: Expansion from case workflows becomes a primary retention driver
supporting: Higher attach rate of compliance services

5_year.focus: Become the operating backbone for accountable municipal offence response.

customerKPI.northStar: Most public-space offence cases in target municipalities start from one digital operating model
supporting: Case quality remains high at scale
Officer burden stays low despite footprint growth

businessKPI.northStar: High-retention workflow revenue anchored in procedural depth and interoperability
supporting: Low churn due to deep operational embedding

milestones: Workflow platform spans multiple European markets

customers.name: Cleanliness Operations Manager
customers.description: Leader of municipal or intermunicipal cleanliness services responsible for dispatching cleanup efficiently, preventing recurrence, and proving service performance under budget pressure.
careAbout: Faster cleanup on real hotspots, not rumor-driven dispatch
Reducing repeat truck rolls and wasted field time
Getting structured handoffs from enforcement teams
Planning relocation of devices based on real recurrence patterns
Showing that service effort improves environmental cleanliness
winsThem: Qualified tasks with enough context for crews to act once
Recurring hotspot maps that guide device moves and crew planning
Cleaner coordination between police, technical services, and contractors
Evidence that the system reduces manual reporting and duplicate work
Executive reporting that credits service teams for outcomes
theirFear: Receiving more alerts but no better prioritization
Being blamed for visible cleanliness issues without better operating data
Running cleanup around a system that stays disconnected from service workflow

jobsToBeDone.name: Remove dumped waste faster while preventing recurrence
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use qualified incident and hotspot data to send crews where they matter most and decide when prevention or relocation is needed.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Service teams clear sites faster, waste fewer visits, and help keep hotspots from bouncing back immediately.

steps.description: Convert qualified incidents into tasks that contain the context crews need before traveling.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Field Service Task Dispatch
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Creates structured work items instead of ad hoc calls and emails.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Officer Mobile Workflow
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Lets field actors confirm site state and close the loop on action taken.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Guides where hardware should be added, moved, or retired.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Device Health and Fleet Monitoring
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps relocation and service planning grounded in real fleet status.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Outcome Analytics and Benchmarking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows whether site interventions actually reduce recurrence.

children.name: First-visit cleanup success

children.name: 30-day hotspot recurrence

children.name: Relocation decision cycle time
children.description: Reduce the time to decide whether to move or keep a device from 28 to 10 days.

top.name: Service productivity gain
top.description: Raise service productivity from 51 to 79 by cutting repeat visits and fragmented reporting.

children.name: Repeat truck rolls per hotspot
children.description: Reduce repeat truck rolls on active hotspots from 2.6 to 1.2.

children.name: Crew hours spent on low-value reports

children.name: Manual cross-team status updates
children.description: Reduce manual handoff updates from 120 to 35 per month.

children.description: Improve recovered value coverage of cleanup spend from 22% to 55%.
